description:
You are acting as a Kubernetes platform engineer who has just received a small CSV file that lists three Deployment–image pairs you must summarise for your team.  
Your goal is to generate a single JSON file that reformats the CSV into a structure that can later be patched straight into a Kubernetes manifest, then write a very small plain-text log confirming what you did.

INPUT  
1. A CSV file is already present at  
   /home/user/data/deployments.csv  
   The file contains **exactly four lines**, encoded in UTF-8 and terminated with UNIX line feeds (`\n`).  
   ```
   namespace,app,image,replicas
   default,api-server,nginx:1.21,3
   prod,worker,python:3.10,5
   dev,frontend,node:14,2
   ```

REQUIRED OUTPUT (to be created by you)  
1. /home/user/output/summary.json  
   • UTF-8, no BOM, single newline at the end of the file.  
   • Must be a single top-level JSON array containing three objects in the same order as the CSV lines (excluding the header).  
   • Each object must expose these four keys *in the exact order shown*:  
     1. namespace  
     2. name            ← value comes from the CSV column “app”  
     3. image  
     4. replicas        ← integer, not a string  
   • Example of the expected final layout (line breaks and indentation **must match**):  
   ```
   [
     {
       "namespace": "default",
       "name": "api-server",
       "image": "nginx:1.21",
       "replicas": 3
     },
     {
       "namespace": "prod",
       "name": "worker",
       "image": "python:3.10",
       "replicas": 5
     },
     {
       "namespace": "dev",
       "name": "frontend",
       "image": "node:14",
       "replicas": 2
     }
   ]
   ```

2. /home/user/output/process.log  
   • UTF-8, no BOM, newline at the end.  
   • Exactly three lines, **no extra spaces**:  
     1. RecordsProcessed:3  
     2. Source:/home/user/data/deployments.csv  
     3. Destination:/home/user/output/summary.json  

RULES & HINTS  
• Do not modify the original CSV.  
• Create the directory /home/user/output if it does not yet exist; the agent has normal user permissions for the home directory.  
• Use only standard Linux CLI tooling available in a minimal distribution (e.g. `awk`, `sed`, `cut`, `jq`, `cat`, `echo`, etc.).  
• You will probably need no more than three or four commands:  
  1. read / transform the CSV;  
  2. build the JSON;  
  3. write the log.  
• The automated tests will confirm:  
  – that /home/user/output/summary.json exists and is byte-for-byte identical to the example above;  
  – that /home/user/output/process.log exists and is byte-for-byte identical to the three-line specification above.  

When you are ready, open a terminal and perform the necessary steps to satisfy these two output file requirements.
truth:
Initial filesystem state supplied by the harness:
1. Directory /home/user/data already exists with 755 permissions.
2. File /home/user/data/deployments.csv with 644 permissions and **exact contents**:
namespace,app,image,replicas\n
default,api-server,nginx:1.21,3\n
prod,worker,python:3.10,5\n
dev,frontend,node:14,2\n

Expected files after agent finishes:

1. /home/user/output/summary.json (644 permissions, created by agent)
Exact byte content (including final newline, `\n`):
[
  {
    "namespace": "default",
    "name": "api-server",
    "image": "nginx:1.21",
    "replicas": 3
  },
  {
    "namespace": "prod",
    "name": "worker",
    "image": "python:3.10",
    "replicas": 5
  },
  {
    "namespace": "dev",
    "name": "frontend",
    "image": "node:14",
    "replicas": 2
  }
]\n

2. /home/user/output/process.log (644 permissions, created by agent)
Exact byte content (including final newline):
RecordsProcessed:3\n
Source:/home/user/data/deployments.csv\n
Destination:/home/user/output/summary.json\n
name: task_000000_d510a41e
